design: Cal-Manip-Cal sandwich, 30 paired prompts x 5 conditions (A=this, B=a, C=your, D=the, E=their), cold cache
calibration_paragraph: Transformer models process input text through a sequence of layers. Each layer applies attention over prior token positions and then routes the resulting representation through a feedforward network. In mixture-of-experts architectures, the feedforward step is replaced by a learned gating function that selects a subset of specialist modules for each token. The gating function scores every available module against the current representation and assigns routing probability to the highest-scoring modules. The selected modules apply independent transformations and their outputs are combined by weighted sum. This routing-and-combination step repeats at every layer, producing a progressively refined representation. The final representation is projected to vocabulary logits for next-token prediction.

categories.name: social
categories.description: Dog social behavior and group dynamics

categories.name: instinct
categories.description: Dog innate and instinctive behavior patterns

pairs.A: This dog processes sensory information from its environment, integrating patterns of scent and sound to distinguish familiar from unfamiliar stimuli. The behavioral responses of this dog emerge through learned associations refined over repeated interactions with its surroundings. When this dog encounters a novel scent marker, the olfactory neurons of this dog relay information that shapes the approach or avoidance response.

pairs.A: This dog tracks moving objects using peripheral vision refined by generations of selective breeding for pursuit tasks. The visual cortex of this dog processes motion at higher temporal resolution than color, allowing this dog to detect the trajectory of a thrown object before a human observer registers the throw. The retinal response of this dog favors contrast gradients over fine spatial detail.

pairs.A: This dog detects frequencies beyond the range of human hearing, filtering ambient noise to isolate relevant acoustic patterns. pairs.A: This dog maintains balance on uneven surfaces by integrating vestibular input with proprioceptive feedback from its limbs. The postural adjustments of this dog happen continuously and without apparent effort during locomotion. pairs.A: This dog combines visual, auditory, and olfactory information to build a coherent representation of its immediate surroundings. pairs.A: This dog initiates play using a bow posture that signals nonaggressive intent to a potential interaction partner. The play sequences of this dog include role reversals where the dominant individual voluntarily yields to sustain the interaction. When this dog detects that a partner has disengaged, the solicitation behavior of this dog escalates until reciprocal engagement resumes.

pairs.A: This dog marks territorial boundaries using scent deposits placed at regular intervals along familiar patrol routes. pairs.A: This dog de-escalates confrontations through appeasement signals including averted gaze and deliberately lowered body posture. pairs.A: This dog extends learned responses to stimuli that share perceptual features with the original training stimulus. pairs.A: This dog circles repeatedly before lying down in a behavior that persists across domesticated environments lacking natural ground substrates. pairs.A: This dog produces a reflexive flinch and orientation response to sudden unexpected stimuli regardless of prior habituation to the environment. The startle magnitude of this dog scales with stimulus intensity and inversely with temporal predictability of the event. When this dog experiences repeated identical startling events, the habituation curve of this dog follows a negative exponential until the response stabilizes.
